title: New Jersey Turnpike Authority v. Devon T. Barber
parties: New Jersey Turnpike Authority v. Devon T. Barber
summary: Civil toll-collection action in the Superior Court of New Jersey, Special Civil Part, involving asserted defenses, counterclaims, and ongoing discovery, including interrogatories and requests for admissions.
description: Civil toll-debt collection action currently pending in the Special Civil Part. The matter includes asserted defenses and counterclaims, along with formal discovery proceedings, including interrogatories and requests for admissions.
sourceNotes: Documents obtained via public records requests and court filings.

timeline.title: Complaint Filed
timeline.description: Civil rights action initiated in Superior Court

timeline.title: Key Evidence Produced
timeline.description: BWC footage and IA investigation documents provided

overview: Federal and state civil rights claims arising from alleged constitutional violations during police encounter. Plaintiff seeks compensatory and punitive damages for unlawful detention, excessive force, and related claims. Discovery is ongoing with key evidence including body-worn camera footage and internal affairs investigations.

reviewNotes.damagesInjuries: Primarily economic exposure relating to disputed toll charges and associated administrative fees. No personal injury claims. Financial impact centers on statutory fee structure and proportionality relative to base toll amounts.
reviewNotes.keyEvidenceSources: Toll transaction records, account statements, statutory authority under N.J.S.A. 27:23-34.3, billing history, and formal discovery responses including interrogatories and requests for admissions regarding administrative fee calculation and cost basis.
reviewNotes.deadlinesLimitations: Special Civil Part procedural timelines apply. Action currently pending. Discovery ongoing. No statute of limitations concerns at this stage.
reviewNotes.reliefSought: Defendant seeks reduction or elimination of disputed administrative fees where unsupported by statutory authority or individualized cost basis. Preservation of defenses and counterclaims as permitted under court rules.
reviewNotes.notes: Case presents statutory interpretation and fee-structure issues rather than factual dispute over toll passage. Discovery aimed at clarifying whether administrative assessments reflect standardized policy versus documented administrative cost. Matter may be suitable for motion practice depending on discovery responses and proportionality analysis.

contingencyChecklist.label: Liability clearly established
contingencyChecklist.notes: BWC footage and witness statements support claims

contingencyChecklist.label: Damages documented and quantifiable
contingencyChecklist.notes: Medical records and expert reports available

contingencyChecklist.label: Statute of limitations satisfied
contingencyChecklist.notes: NTC filed timely, complaint filed within SOL
